title: Chapter 16 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)
content:

{{Quote|Padmé is worried about Yoda, and she's not one to stay in one place as instructed. She leaves the safety of her ship and heads out into the cold with C-3PO and R2-D2 to find him. As they approach the cave, they must tangle with invisible droid soldiers.|StarWars.com summary|<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/16.html|text=''Clone Wars'': Chapter Sixteen (small)}}</ref>}}
"'''Chapter 16'''" is the sixteenth episode of the ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' animated television series, the sixth episode in [[Clone Wars: Season Two|Season Two]], and the sixteenth episode in [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|Volume One]]. Directed by [[Genndy Tartakovsky]], the episode originally aired on [[Cartoon Network]] on [[April 2]], [[2004]].

==Plot summary==
[[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gregar Typho/Legends|Gregar Typho]] watch the Jedi ice cave with their [[Electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]]. Amidala and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] are concerned that [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] has not returned, but Typho and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] assure her that the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] will be fine. When Typho returns to the ship to check the communications array, Amidala heads off after Master Yoda, telling the two [[Droid/Legends|droids]] that they will accompany her. Artoo's sensors pick up [[Spelunker probe droid/Legends|multiple objects]] approaching, and the trio dive for cover as a barrage of laser fire blasts out of the blizzard. Amidala throws her cloak out as a decoy and then fires her [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], destroying the cloaked [[Chameleon droid/Legends|chameleon droid]]. However, Artoo says that there are two more. With this, she asks 3PO if he can get her coat but actually carelessly using him as a decoy. All of a sudden, the other two appear and open fire upon C-3PO, while Amidala uses a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] to destroy the remaining two droids.

Master Yoda appears out of the snowstorm with [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]] and [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]]. The Jedi are safe, but the cave temple has been nearly destroyed. Master Yoda wonders how the temple's location was known. Artoo patches into one of the chameleon droids and provides the answer: [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]].

==Continuity==
The appearance of the [[J-type 327 Nubian royal starship/Legends|J-type 327 Nubian royal starship]] is a blooper, as the ship was intended to be a [[H-type Nubian yacht/Legends|H-type Nubian yacht]], which is seen in the two previous episodes.

This episode contains a variation on the [[Wikipedia:Lawrence of Arabia (film)|''Lawrence of Arabia'']] musical theme. It can be heard when [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], along with [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]] and [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]], emerge out of the snow on the horizon.

''Clone Wars'' producer [[Genndy Tartakovsky]] revealed in his [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] audio commentary on [[StarWars.com]] and on the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|''Star Wars: Clone Wars'' Volume One]] DVD that he designed [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] the way he did and animated C-3PO's eyes in a way that pays homage to the animators of and the animation style of [[Nelvana]], who were the production company behind the animated segment from ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'' and the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Droids'']] and [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated series. Tartakovsky explains that he made his version of the character similar to Nelvana's.
